title: Canadian Apprenticeship Strategy – Investments in Training Equipment - Who can apply - Canada.ca
content:
- How we assess your application
Your organization must be one of the following types:
- a union representing workers in the Red Seal trades
- an organization managing training trust funds for unions representing workers in the Red Seal trades, for example: a joint training trust fund set up by a union a joint training trust fund set up by a union and an employer association
- a joint training trust fund set up by a union
- a joint training trust fund set up by a union and an employer association
- a training provider that provides technical training to pre-apprentices and apprentices as part of a recognized apprenticeship program for a Red Seal trade
Note to organizations located in Quebec
Organizations in the Province of Quebec can’t apply for this funding opportunity. ESDC is providing the Government of Quebec with funding so that they can implement similar activities that account for the unique features of Quebec's apprenticeship system.
Note to organizations located in Alberta New
Organizations that are located and operating in the Province of Alberta should consult Alberta Municipal Affairs to ensure their application complies with the Provincial Priorities Act (PPA). It is the applicant’s responsibility to obtain the required authorization before entering into an agreement with the Government of Canada.

### Projects and activities
Your project must meet all the following criteria:
- target training for pre-apprentices and apprentices in the Red Seal trades *Note : Projects must target training for apprentices. Pre-apprentices can be included in the project but are not required.
*Note : Projects must target training for apprentices. Pre-apprentices can be included in the project but are not required.
- support trades that are designated under the Interprovincial Standard Red Seal Program
- request no more than $5,000,000 *Note: organizations can receive up to $5,000,000 in funding in a fiscal year under this continuous intake
*Note: organizations can receive up to $5,000,000 in funding in a fiscal year under this continuous intake
- have cash contributions from sources other than the Government of Canada: equal to or more than 50% of the cost of new, up-to-date equipment and materials 30% if you can clearly demonstrate that a minimum of 50% of your project’s activities are affected by any of the following challenges: remote location limited access to infrastructure, or limited partnership opportunities
- equal to or more than 50% of the cost of new, up-to-date equipment and materials
- 30% if you can clearly demonstrate that a minimum of 50% of your project’s activities are affected by any of the following challenges: remote location limited access to infrastructure, or limited partnership opportunities
- limited access to infrastructure, or
- limited partnership opportunities

### Determine the minimum cash contribution required to meet eligibility criteria
This tool only provides an estimate.
To be eligible, your project must have a cash contribution equal to or more than 50% of the cost of equipment and materials including shipping and installation New (including GST/HST) .
- This must come from sources other than the Government of Canada
- Only cash contributions are eligible under this funding process. In-kind contributions are not applicable.
Note: If your organization receives a Canada Revenue Agency (CRA) tax rebate for the purchase of equipment and materials, this amount should be deducted from the total cost. It is your responsibility to claim your CRA tax rebate.
If your project experiences challenges
We’ll accept a 30% cash contribution if you can clearly demonstrate that a minimum of 50% of your project's activities are affected by any of the following challenges:
- limited access to infrastructure
- limited partnership opportunities

#### Estimate the minimum cash contribution you would need to provide for the purchase of equipment and materials.
With an equipment and materials total cost of $ and a minimum cash contribution of 50 % , you would need to provide:
- A minimum of $ in cash contributions

#### See example calculations
- You are purchasing a crane for $400,000 (including tax)
- Shipping and installation costs are $50,000
- Your costs to support reporting are $2,500 (cannot exceed $3,900)
- Your total project cost is the cost of the equipment and materials, the shipping and installation and the cost of reporting: $400,000 + $50,000 + $2,500 = $452,500
$400,000 + $50,000 + $2,500 = $452,500
- You are providing a 50% cash contribution You would need to provide a minimum of $225,000 in cash contributions $450,000 x 0.5 (50%) = $225,000 Your request to ESDC would be for a maximum of $227,500 $225,000 + $2,500 = $227,500
You would need to provide a minimum of $225,000 in cash contributions
$450,000 x 0.5 (50%) = $225,000
Your request to ESDC would be for a maximum of $227,500
$225,000 + $2,500 = $227,500
- You are purchasing the following equipment (all prices include tax): An excavator for $345,500 A dozer for $525,000 A haul truck for $822,550 A backhoe for $123,598
- An excavator for $345,500
- A dozer for $525,000
- A haul truck for $822,550
- A backhoe for $123,598
- The total of your equipment purchase is $1,816,648
- Your costs to support reporting are $3,900 (cannot exceed $3,900)
- You are providing a 50% cash contribution You would need to provide a minimum of $908,324 in cash contributions $1,816,648 X 0.50 (50%) = $908,324 Your request to ESDC would be for a maximum of $912,224 $908,324 + $3,900 = $912,224
You would need to provide a minimum of $908,324 in cash contributions
$1,816,648 X 0.50 (50%) = $908,324
Your request to ESDC would be for a maximum of $912,224
$908,324 + $3,900 = $912,224
- You are purchasing the following equipment and materials (all prices include tax), that includes shipping and installation costs: 2 X Battery-powered Duotack Applicator - $4,536 1 X Infrared Thermometer - $142 8 X Toolbox - $182 8 X Tape Measures - $299 8 X Utility Knife 25mm Super Duty - $146 8 X Shingler’s Hatchet - $503 5 X Hot Air Hand Tool - $4,893 8 X Laptop - $11,339 8 X Office Productivity Software - $1,554 Shipping of equipment and materials: $2,000 Installation of equipment: $1,000
- 2 X Battery-powered Duotack Applicator - $4,536
- 1 X Infrared Thermometer - $142
- 8 X Tape Measures - $299
- 8 X Utility Knife 25mm Super Duty - $146
- 8 X Shingler’s Hatchet - $503
- 5 X Hot Air Hand Tool - $4,893
- 8 X Laptop - $11,339
- 8 X Office Productivity Software - $1,554
- Shipping of equipment and materials: $2,000
- Installation of equipment: $1,000
- The total of your equipment purchase is $26,594
- Your costs to support reporting are $3,900 (cannot exceed $3,900)
- You are providing a 30% cash contribution because at least 50% of your project's activities are delivered in a remote location You would need to provide a minimum of $7,978 in cash contributions $26,594 X 0.30 (30%) = $7,978 Your request to ESDC would be for a maximum of $22,516 $26,594 X 0.70 (70%) = $18,616 (ESDC contribution) $18,616 + $3,900 = $22,516
You would need to provide a minimum of $7,978 in cash contributions
$26,594 X 0.30 (30%) = $7,978
Your request to ESDC would be for a maximum of $22,516
$26,594 X 0.70 (70%) = $18,616 (ESDC contribution)
$18,616 + $3,900 = $22,516

### What the funding can be used to pay for
We can only pay you back for eligible costs. Eligible costs must directly relate to your project activities.
- purchase of equipment (new or refurbished) and materials directly related to training pre-apprentices and apprentices in a designated Red Seal trade
- the collection of data and reporting of results
- Training equipment and materials that meet industry standards applicable to the job site. Refurbished equipment that aligns with industry standards is eligible . Examples of training equipment and materials: technology, including software and simulators, for example: a tower crane backhoe virtual reality simulator advanced welding technology geothermal training systems milling/bevelling guns electric hoists sheet metal for use in training activities (for example, welding) trailers to transport equipment for mobile classrooms in remote locations electric vehicles used exclusively to train automotive service technicians computers, tablets, and laptops used exclusively to operate technology for training purposes only mandatory protective equipment for training purposes only
- Examples of training equipment and materials: technology, including software and simulators, for example: a tower crane backhoe virtual reality simulator advanced welding technology geothermal training systems milling/bevelling guns electric hoists sheet metal for use in training activities (for example, welding) trailers to transport equipment for mobile classrooms in remote locations electric vehicles used exclusively to train automotive service technicians computers, tablets, and laptops used exclusively to operate technology for training purposes only mandatory protective equipment for training purposes only
- technology, including software and simulators, for example: a tower crane backhoe virtual reality simulator
- virtual reality simulator
- advanced welding technology
- geothermal training systems
- milling/bevelling guns
- sheet metal for use in training activities (for example, welding)
- trailers to transport equipment for mobile classrooms in remote locations
- electric vehicles used exclusively to train automotive service technicians
- computers, tablets, and laptops used exclusively to operate technology for training purposes only
- mandatory protective equipment for training purposes only
- shipping and delivery of equipment
- installation of equipment to make it operational, including electrical upgrades
- staff wages or professional fees (including mandatory employment-related costs) to support reporting only (a maximum of $3,900 per project can be requested)
- purchase or lease of used equipment
- transportation, such as: vehicle (excludes electric vehicles used to train automotive service technicians) trailer to transport equipment (excludes mobile classrooms used for remote locations)
- vehicle (excludes electric vehicles used to train automotive service technicians)
- trailer to transport equipment (excludes mobile classrooms used for remote locations)
- development and delivery of training program and related learning materials, such as: workbooks digital content
- site preparation for installation
- warranties and maintenance of equipment
- storage, such as: cabinets and shelving tool storage outdoor storage
- cabinets and shelving
- training facility improvement or expansion, such as: renovations and upgrades building expansions facility renovations
- renovations and upgrades
- facility renovations
- staff wages for the development and delivery of in-person, online and virtual training
- set-up of classrooms (excludes mobile classrooms used for remote locations), including: office supplies desks and furniture computers, laptops and tablets
- computers, laptops and tablets
- purchase or lease of real property, such as buildings or land

### Other eligibility requirements
- application is completed in full
- attestation in part 4 is checked
- a quote is provided from each vendor for the purchase of training equipment and materials including shipping and installation New
We may ask you to provide the following documents if you are:
- a union: a copy of your active relevant collective agreements representing Red Seal trades workers
- a copy of your active relevant collective agreements representing Red Seal trades workers
- an organization managing training trust funds for unions representing workers in the Red Seal trades: a copy of an active agreement with a union representing Red Seal trades workers, and documentation that demonstrates the union’s eligibility, such as a copy of active relevant collective agreements representing Red Seal trades workers
- a copy of an active agreement with a union representing Red Seal trades workers, and
- documentation that demonstrates the union’s eligibility, such as a copy of active relevant collective agreements representing Red Seal trades workers
- a training provider: copies of current course offerings that clearly indicate: technical training for Red Seal trades apprenticeships – including the trade name and the level of technical training information regarding the number of apprentices in Red Seal trades trained annually an attestation letter from the apprenticeship training authority in the jurisdiction where you operate
- copies of current course offerings that clearly indicate: technical training for Red Seal trades apprenticeships – including the trade name and the level of technical training information regarding the number of apprentices in Red Seal trades trained annually an attestation letter from the apprenticeship training authority in the jurisdiction where you operate
- technical training for Red Seal trades apprenticeships – including the trade name and the level of technical training
- information regarding the number of apprentices in Red Seal trades trained annually
- an attestation letter from the apprenticeship training authority in the jurisdiction where you operate

## How we assess your application
We may refuse applications that are incomplete or contain errors
We will contact you to request any mandatory information if it is missing from your application. We will do this before we determine if your application is eligible. If you receive such a request, you must respond within 5 business days of the date we sent the request. If you do not respond before the deadline, we will review your application with the information on file. This could result in your application being screened out.

### Assessing for eligibility
We will assess the eligibility of your application, based on whether your:
- organization is eligible
- application package is complete, including application for funding form budget detail template quote from all vendors for the purchase of training equipment and materials
- application for funding form
- budget detail template
- quote from all vendors for the purchase of training equipment and materials
- project targets training for pre-apprentices and apprentices in the Red Seal trades, including the number of participants expected to be reached *Note : Projects must target training for apprentices. Pre-apprentices can be included in the project but are not required.
*Note : Projects must target training for apprentices. Pre-apprentices can be included in the project but are not required.
- project activities are eligible and support the program objective of purchasing equipment or material that will enhance the quality of training of pre-apprentices and apprentices
- expenditures are eligible and support project activities
- project supports trades that are designated under the Interprovincial Standard Red Seal Program
- cash contribution from sources other than the Government of Canada is equal to or more than 50%. If your cash contribution is equal to 30% you must provide a justification.
It is important to submit a complete application. All eligibility requirements must be met to be considered for funding. As priorities may change over time, please visit this page regularly to stay updated on eligibility requirements and priorities.
However, we may give priority to projects that:
- are submitted by unions representing workers in the Red Seal trades or organizations managing training trust funds for unions representing workers in the Red Seal trades, for example: a joint training trust fund set up by a union, or a union and an employer association
- a joint training trust fund set up by a union, or
- a union and an employer association
- purchase equipment and materials that support the development of environmental skills for pre-apprentices and apprentices in Red Seal trades
- support the training of pre-apprentices and apprentices in one of 30 Red Seal trades linked to the residential construction sector *Note : Projects must target training for apprentices. Pre-apprentices can be included in the project but are not required.
*Note : Projects must target training for apprentices. Pre-apprentices can be included in the project but are not required.
- have activities in a variety of regions (except Quebec). This includes projects with activities taking place in remote locations.
- support current and emerging Government of Canada priorities (as these may change, please visit this page regularly to stay updated on current priorities)
